title: Fox-Sudakov Conjecture (Conjecture 1.2)
statement_text: For every graph $H$ there exists $c>0$ such that for every $\varepsilon$ with $0<\varepsilon\leq 1/2$ and every $H$-free graph $G$, there exists $X\subseteq V(G)$ with $|X|\geq\varepsilon^{c}|G|$ such that one of $G[X],\overline{G}[X]$ has edge-density at most $\varepsilon$.
context_text: Fox and Sudakov proposed this conjecture (Conjecture 7.1 in [10]) as a polynomial-dependence strengthening of Rödl's theorem on induced subgraph density. It implies the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture and had not been verified for any nontrivial graph $H$ prior to this work.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for $H=P_4$ with the optimal $\delta=\varepsilon$ (and a stronger maximum-degree bound), and more generally for all graphs $H$ obtainable by vertex-substitution from copies of $P_4$ and its subgraphs, via the stronger 'viral' property.
notes: Conjecture 7.1 of Fox–Sudakov (2008). A footnote in the paper notes that subsequent work by Bucić, Fox and Pham [4] (arXiv:2403.08303) has since shown that all graphs satisfying the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture also satisfy the Fox-Sudakov conjecture.
paper_title: Induced subgraph density. II. Sparse and dense sets in cographs
